assertions.description: Output contains numbered slides with headlines (max 8 words each) and body text (max 25 words per slide), each slide clearly separated and labeled
Slide 1 is a hook (question, claim, or statistic) and the final slide contains a specific CTA beyond just 'follow for more'
An accompanying caption of 75-200 words is included that works as standalone text

assertion_results.note: 7 slides with clear headlines under 8 words, body text under 25 words each
Slide 1 opens with provocative question; slide 7 has specific CTA linking to guide
6 slides properly separated with headlines and body text within limits
Slide 1 hooks with cost statistic; final slide directs to article/contact
Caption at 89 words for LinkedIn register
8 slides with clear structure, EU-specific terminology handled well
Opens with comparison claim about funding models; closes with save CTA
Caption at 134 words provides standalone context about public broadcasting

methodology: Binary assertion testing: 3 inputs (standard, minimal, European context) x 3 assertions per skill. Each assertion graded pass/fail based on whether generated output satisfies the requirement.
